zh.Bow of Fife, Fife, Scotland: 蘇格蘭費伏包福 本 費伏
zh.lifeSummary: 當Aethelred Lay Abbot of Dunkeld於1072年出生在蘇格蘭費伏登福姆林時，他的父親Malcolm III King of Scotland41歲，母親St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland27歲。他於大約1088年與Tul or Orlin MacDuff, Princess of Scotland在蘇格蘭費伏結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1093年在蘇格蘭費伏享年21歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭。

zh-hans.Bow of Fife, Fife, Scotland: 苏格兰費伏包福 本 費伏
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Aethelred Lay Abbot of Dunkeld于1072年出生在苏格兰費伏登福姆林时，他的父亲Malcolm III King of Scotland41岁，母亲St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland27岁。他于大约1088年与Tul or Orlin MacDuff, Princess of Scotland在苏格兰費伏结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。他去世于1093年在苏格兰費伏享年21岁，埋葬在苏格兰。

nameMeanings.name: Lay
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Scottish: shortened form of   McClay  .
German: habitational name from any of the places so named, in the Rhineland near Koblenz and in Bavaria, from lay(h), a word meaning ‘stone, rock, slate’.

variants.name: Lee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sang, Jae, Jong, Jung, Sung, Yong, Kyung, Seung, Dong, Kwang, Myung.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a meadow or a patch of arable land, Middle English lee, lea, from Old English lēa, dative case (used after a preposition) of lēah, which originally meant ‘wood or glade’.

English: habitational name from any of the many places in England named with Old English lēah ‘wood, glade’, including Lee in Buckinghamshire, Essex, Hampshire, Kent, and Shropshire, and Lea in Cheshire, Derbyshire, Herefordshire, Lancashire, Lincolnshire, and Wiltshire.

nameMeanings.name: Abbot
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of Abbott . The spelling with one -t is more typically Scottish than English, and in Gaelic-speaking areas of Scotland, this is sometimes an Anglicized form of McNabb .

variants.name: Abbott
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English abbod, abbot(t), abbat ‘abbot’ (Old English abbod) or Old French abet ‘priest’. Both the Old English and the Old French term are derived from Late Latin abbas (genitive abbatis) ‘priest’, from Greek abbas, from Aramaic aba ‘father’. The nickname was presumably a joking reference to a person's behaviour. In the US, the English name is also sometimes a translation of a cognate or equivalent European name, e.g. Italian Abate , Spanish Abad , or German Abt .

History: George Abbot from Yorkshire, England, settled in Andover, MA, in 1640; he had numerous prominent descendants. George Abbott (probably not the same man) died in Rowley, MA, in 1647. Another early migrant was James Abbott, who came from Somerset, England, to Long Island, NY, in the 17th century.

variants.name: Freer
meanings: English: sometimes from Middle English frere, frier ‘brother, friend, comrade’ (Old French frere). During the 13th century, however, the word increasingly denoted ‘member of one of the mendicant religious orders’, the usual modern sense of the word friar. The first friars arrived in England in 1224. As with Abbot , Monk , Nunn , and Prior , late medieval use of Frere as a nickname was probably uncomplimentary, for by the mid 14th century the once popular friars had become a byword in satirical literature for self-indulgent, smooth-talking hypocrisy. Friars were not allowed to marry, but some of them probably fathered children. After the Reformation former friars were free to marry, and one such is said to have been the progenitor of the Friers of Melrose parish, Roxburgh, an instance, if true, of post-medieval surname creation. The name may also be occupational, for someone who lived or worked at a friary (Middle English frere ‘house of friars’).
Americanized form of French Frère (see Frere ).
German: cognate of   Friedrich  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
Chinese:
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Dunkeld
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: nickname from Middle Low German dunkel, a byform of dunker ‘dark, conceited, unclear’, also applied as a byname for a blind person. Compare Dunckel and Dunkle .

variants.name: Dunckel
meanings: German: variant of   Dunkel  .

variants.name: Dunkle
meanings: Americanized form of German   Dunkel  .

nameMeanings.name: Aethelred
meanings: A male name, in Anglo-Saxon found written Æthelred, from Anglo-Saxon ethel-red, noble counsel, or noble counsellor.

en.lifeSummary: When Aethelred Lay Abbot of Dunkeld was born in 1072, in Dunfermline, Fife, Scotland, his father, Malcolm III King of Scotland, was 41 and his mother, St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland, was 27. He married Tul or Orlin MacDuff, Princess of Scotland about 1088, in Fife, Scotland. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died in 1093, in Fife, Scotland, at the age of 21, and was buried in Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Aethelred Lay Abbot of Dunkeld est né en 1072, à Dunfermline, Fife, Écosse, son père, Malcolm III King of Scotland, avait 41 ans et sa mère, St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland, avait 27 ans. Il a épousé Tul or Orlin MacDuff, Princess of Scotland environ 1088, à Fife, Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé en 1093, à Fife, Écosse, à l’âge de 21 ans, et a été enterré à Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Aethelred Lay Abbot of Dunkeld 1072 in Dunfermline, Fife, Scotland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Malcolm III King of Scotland 41 und seine Mutter St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland 27. Er hat um 1088 in Fife, Scotland Tul or Orlin MacDuff, Princess of Scotland geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb 1093 im Alter von 21 Jahren in Fife, Scotland und wurde in Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Aethelred Lay Abbot of Dunkeld è nato il nel 1072 nel Dunfermline, Fife, Scotland, il suo padre, Malcolm III King of Scotland, aveva 41 anni e il suo madre, St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland, aveva 27 anni. Lui sposato Tul or Orlin MacDuff, Princess of Scotland circa 1088, nel Fife, Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì nel 1093, nel Fife, Scotland, all'età di 21 anni, e fu sepolto a Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Aethelred Lay Abbot of Dunkeldが1072年にスコットランドフィフダンフエルムラインで生まれた時，父Malcolm III King of Scotlandは41歳，母St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotlandは27歳であった。約1088年にスコットランドフィフでTul or Orlin MacDuff, Princess of Scotlandと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼は1093年にスコットランドフィフで21歳で死去し，スコットランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Aethelred Lay Abbot of Dunkeld(이)가 1072년 스컷랜드 피프 던퍼라이인에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Malcolm III King of Scotland(은)는 41세였고, 그의 어머니 St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland(은)는 27세였다. 그는 대략 1088년 스컷랜드 피프에서 Tul or Orlin MacDuff, Princess of Scotland(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1093년 스컷랜드 피프에서 21세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Aethelred Lay Abbot of Dunkeld nasceu em 1072, em Dunfermline, Fife, Scotland, seu pai, Malcolm III King of Scotland, tinha 41 anos e sua mãe, St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland, tinha 27 anos. Ele casou-se com Tul or Orlin MacDuff, Princess of Scotland aproximadamente 1088, em Fife, Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1093, em Fife, Scotland, com 21 anos, e foi sepultado em Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Aethelred Lay Abbot of Dunkeld родился в 1072 г. в местности дунфирмлаин, фиф, Шотландия, его отцу по имени Malcolm III King of Scotland было 41 год, а матери, которую звали St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland, 27. Он женился на женщине по имени Tul or Orlin MacDuff, Princess of Scotland около 1088 г. в местности фиф, Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер в 1093 г. в местности фиф, Шотландия в возрасте 21 год и был похоронен в местности Шотландия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Aethelred Lay Abbot of Dunkeld nació en 1072, en Dunfermline, Fife, Scotland, su papá, Malcolm III King of Scotland, tenía 41 años y su mamá, St Margaret of Wessex, Queen of Scotland, tenía 27 años. Se casó con Tul or Orlin MacDuff, Princess of Scotland aproximadamente 1088, en Fife, Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió en 1093, en Fife, Scotland, a la edad de 21 años, y fue sepultado en Scotland.
